Transcrição Completa: Day 11 - Efficiency

 

Conversa

 

[00:00] "Day 11, we're going to talk about efficiency. So, in real time, you might be like, 'Jeremy, I'm trying to hit 100, but dude, like it's a lot of shit. How am I supposed to hit a hundred if I have to like, dude, this email takes like 20 30 minutes sometimes?' Like, Jeremy, I mean, I'm on a call. Or like, dude, I have I have this going on and that going on, but Jeremy, like, I got to do this."

 

[00:25] "Hear me out. You have 24 hours in a day, right? Well, guess what? So does every billionaire that's ever existed. You know, Warren Buffett didn't have shit. He owns banks now. You want to talk about not having time for some email? It's called efficiencies. I'm going to teach you something real quick. You can also go into profit and prosper and watch more on efficiencies."

 

[00:50] "Here it is in real time. Your ability to take every single thing around you that you're already doing and make it faster, make it more productive for you. Make it more efficient. Make the process specific to you. Exactly what I just said, more specific to you so it becomes more beneficial, more fast, or whatever the fuck words you want to use here that's going to get you a better result than you're doing now from a perception misalignment."

 

[01:20] "You can recalibrate your perspective with this efficiencies viewpoint. Add this on top of everything you do. I swear to God, literally hit your 100 per day. There's no excuses. Doesn't matter what you want to tell yourself. That's just an inefficiency to even have that thought."

 

[01:38] "The efficiency mindset itself is all about overcoming every single thing in real time, no matter what. No, no obstacle, no objection, no possible situation on this planet will stop you from hitting your 100. Hit your 100. You have 24 hours in a day. Hit your 100. Does not matter what you possibly say."

 

[02:00] "Seek efficiencies. Make it better. Make it more productive for yourself. Use Loom instead. Send yourself a video. If you're doing follow-up, you got to talk. You hear how I talk? You hear how fast I talked? Do you think I talked this fast for any given reason other than I found that I could speak this fast?"

 

[02:20] "Now I have the ability to have the efficiency of getting every single point that I want to get across while I'm still articulate while I'm also getting every single thing that would have normally taken x amount of time, seconds, minutes, whatever you want to say. And in real time, it takes me this amount of time. Seek out efficiencies."

 

[02:40] "I sold my car. Why did I sell my car? So I can get Ubered around everywhere I go. Why don't I take Uber X? Why do I take Uber Black or Uber Lux or whatever? Uber Select or whatever it is. All of it is nicer than what's at the bottom."

 

[02:55] "Where in real time, if you take an Uber Select, Uber Black, Uber Lux, whatever you can afford, you get to be on the phone in the back while you have a professional driver who comes up in his little suit and is in a BMW or a Tahoe or whatever it is. You get to sit in the back and you get to have in real time conversations. You in real time now have an understanding of what an efficiency is."

 

[03:20] "Some people would be like, 'Oh, Jeremy, I want I want my car, dude. I love driving. I love the music. I love to smoke.' I mean, I don't care. Your 100 sales per day don't care. Everybody that you actually need to reach out to in the life that you want to build in the future, your future self's literally calling back like a time machine."

 

[03:40] "It's like, 'Hey, I I've got you to the point where Jeremy's literally cupping his hands right now, yelling, 'It's not me. I can't I can't talk.' Your future self can't actually come and poke you on the shoulder and be like, 'Hey, you should have hit your 100 per day. We would have got where we are now way faster.'"

 

[04:00] "But guess what? You have me right now waking your ass up. Go hit your 100. Don't make any excuses. Seek out efficiencies. Make it possible. The thought itself to even ask why shows that there's a represented obstacle within your mindset."

 

[04:18] "What is it? Find it, identify it, bust out a piece of paper as an exercise. Write down the excuse and then break down the excuse because it's not the excuse itself of why can't you do it. It's the reasons behind it. Write down the reasons."

 

[04:35] "Are those reasons still pretty ambiguous and broad? Break them down further. Find the psychological pain or the psychological vulnerability or whatever it is in your process or your system that you find is inefficient."

 

 

📊 RESUMO EXECUTIVO

 

NÚMEROS PRINCIPAIS:

Meta diária: 100 ações/vendas por dia

24 horas disponíveis por dia (igual para todos, incluindo bilionários)

Emails podem levar 20-30 minutos (ineficiente)

 

ESTRATÉGIAS E SISTEMAS:

Mentalidade de Eficiência: Transformar cada ação em mais rápida e produtiva

Uso de Loom: Substituir emails longos por vídeos

Falar Rápido: Aumentar velocidade de comunicação mantendo articulação

Uber Select/Black/Lux: Usar transporte com motorista para trabalhar durante deslocamentos

Eliminar Desculpas: Identificar e quebrar objeções mentais

Exercício de Papel: Escrever desculpas e decompor em razões fundamentais

 

ESTRUTURA:

Framework de 24 horas (todos têm o mesmo tempo)

Processo de identificação de ineficiências

Sistema de recalibração de perspectiva

Metodologia de decomposição de obstáculos psicológicos

 

DADOS FINANCEIROS:

Referência a Warren Buffett (bilionário que possui bancos)

Investimento em Uber premium vs. Uber X (priorizar produtividade sobre economia)

Venda de carro para otimizar tempo

 

CONCEITOS-CHAVE:

Eficiência > Conforto

Velocidade de execução

Eliminação de "desalinhamento de percepção"

Futuro-eu chamando do futuro

Zero tolerância para desculpas

 

DURAÇÃO: ~4:50 minutos FORMATO: Monólogo motivacional/treinamento direto
